rxn_class_summary: Electroreductive stereoselective hydrocoupling (homocoupling) of aryl-substituted methyl cinnamates to all-trans cyclized hydrodimers (cyclopentanone-carboxylates) via Dieckmann condensation, enabling asymmetric synthesis of dl-3,4-diarylhydrodimers.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic one-electron reduction of the cinnamate C=C to a substrate-centered anion radical (radical anion), which undergoes homocoupling; performed in an undivided cell at constant current with Pb cathode/Pt anode in MeCN/Et4NOTs.

key_intermediates.name: cinnamate anion radical (one-electron reduced methyl cinnamate)
meso-3,4-diaryladipate diester (meso-3, minor open-chain product not cyclized by base)

electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to the cinnamate to give a β-aryl-stabilized ester anion radical; two such radical anions homocouple C-β to C-β to form a dl-favored 3,4-diaryladipate dianion, which undergoes intramolecular Dieckmann condensation to the all-trans cyclopentanone-carboxylate hydrodimer.
rate_determining_step: Stereodetermining step is the C–C homocoupling of two anion radicals; the relative energies of the two diastereomeric coupling transition states (dl vs meso) set the high dl-selectivity.

probe_reagents.name: methyl cis-cinnamate (cis-1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave same dl/meso = 92/8 stereoselectivity as trans-1a, indicating a common (planar) anion-radical intermediate independent of olefin geometry.

probe_reagents.name: cumarin (coumarin, rigid cyclic cinnamate analogue)
probe_reagents.observation: Electroreduction gave hydrodimer 9 in 66% with reversed (meso-preferred, 50% de) selectivity, attributed to the rigid cyclic constraint; supports that acyclic conformational freedom of the anion radical controls dl-selectivity.

probe_reagents.name: chiral auxiliary [(1R)-exo]-3-exo-(diphenylmethyl)borneol cinnamate (14a)
probe_reagents.observation: DFT/semiempirical optimization (UHF/AM1, UB3LYP/3-21G*, UB3LYP/6-31G*) of its anion radical shows conformer A more stable than B by 3.1–6.0 kcal/mol; Re face is shielded by the CHPh2 group, so homocoupling at Si face gives (R,R)-15a, rationalizing the observed 87–95% ee.

notes: Authors explicitly reject earlier hypotheses invoking unusual interactions between anion radicals and the cathode surface or templating by water (Utley, Nielsen); they argue PM3/AM1 transition-state calculations on the bare anion-radical homocoupling already reproduce dl-preference for cinnamates and meso-preference for coumarin, so no special surface/water templating is required. Dieckmann cyclization to 2 requires sufficiently basic electrolyte conditions; under undivided-cell constant current, meso open-chain diester (meso-3) is not cyclized, accounting for the small (<10%) meso byproduct. Over-reduction to methyl phenylpropionate (4a) is a minor competing pathway.
